summary: Matched lbColl_CopyHitCapsule in src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c by introducing local HitVictim pointer aliases for the two victim arrays on src and dst, then indexing those aliases inside the copy loop. The behavior stayed the same, but the expression shape and array-size source were adjusted to satisfy 100% matching.
what_changed: In lbColl_CopyHitCapsule, added local aliases: sv1 for src->victims_1, dv1 for dst->victims_1, sv2 for src->victims_2, and dv2 for dst->victims_2.

Changed the copy loop body from direct member indexing, dst->victims_1[i] = src->victims_1[i] and dst->victims_2[i] = src->victims_2[i], to pointer-alias indexing, dv1[i] = sv1[i] and dv2[i] = sv2[i].
Changed the loop bound macro argument from ARRAY_SIZE(dst->victims_1) to ARRAY_SIZE(src->victims_1).
No semantic change is evident from the diff; the PR was aimed at matching lbColl_CopyHitCapsule 100%.
smart_moves: Used local pointer aliases for repeated struct member arrays, a common decomp tactic when the compiler appears to keep base addresses in locals/registers rather than repeatedly materializing member accesses.
Kept the loop index as size_t and preserved ARRAY_SIZE instead of hardcoding the victim array length.
Swapped ARRAY_SIZE to the source array, which likely better matched the original expression or compiler output while remaining equivalent if src and dst are the same HitCapsule type.
decomp_lessons: For copy helpers over struct member arrays, direct field indexing may not match even when behavior is correct; introduce typed local pointers to model the compiler's chosen base-address temporaries.
Small source-expression changes, such as which equivalent array is passed to ARRAY_SIZE, can affect matching in decompilation work.

When copying between two instances of the same struct, using source-side and destination-side aliases can make generated code closer to the original compiler's register allocation and addressing pattern.
naming_conventions: The matched function uses the lbColl_ prefix for collision-library functions.
Local aliases used short source/destination prefixes: sv1 and sv2 for source victim arrays, dv1 and dv2 for destination victim arrays.
Existing struct/member names retained HitCapsule, HitVictim, victims_1, victims_2, x44, and x45.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduce local pointer variables for repeated struct member array bases before the loop.
Index pointer aliases inside the loop instead of repeatedly accessing src->member[i] and dst->member[i].
Use an equivalent ARRAY_SIZE expression that better matches expected codegen; here ARRAY_SIZE(src->victims_1) replaced ARRAY_SIZE(dst->victims_1).

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.interpretation: No technical review guidance was recorded; the available comment is positive acknowledgment only.

follow_up_queries: Find other lbcollision.c copy helpers where struct member arrays fail to match until local pointer aliases are introduced.
Search for ARRAY_SIZE argument swaps between equivalent source and destination arrays in matching PRs.
Look for HitCapsule victim array handling patterns involving victims_1 and victims_2.
